action.hypothesis: The current JumpReLU search direction appears locally exhausted, and a gated SAE that separates support selection from reconstruction magnitude may unlock a lower-FVU regime at K=128 by learning support with an explicit sigmoid routing branch while keeping the strong baseline optimization settings.
action.summary: Stop minor JumpReLU sweeps and switch to a proxy gated-family prototype at K=128 using the current best training regime as the comparison anchor.
action.self_review: This follows the severe-stagnation requirement to move to a fundamentally different architecture after repeated no-improvement rounds on JumpReLU. The main risk is another gated-family failure, but the existing implementation path makes a clean architecture probe more justified than continuing exhausted local tuning.
notes_to_memory: Five consecutive non-improving rounds indicate the local JumpReLU sweep is exhausted for now.
This round switches to the incubating gated family as a fundamentally different encoder-routing approach while preserving the strongest known optimization settings for a fair comparison.
A clean gated proxy result is needed before deciding whether to stabilize the family or abandon it in favor of another architecture direction.
next_hypotheses: If gated improves proxy FVU at acceptable throughput, keep the family alive and tune gate-specific parameters such as temperature or init_logit in a stabilization follow-up.
If gated quality is promising but runtime regresses, do a code/performance follow-up rather than concluding the architecture is bad.
If gated crashes again or is clearly worse, retire this family and move to a different architecture branch instead of returning to minor JumpReLU tweaks.
